title: The value of a complex expression should only be cast to a type that is narrower and of the same signedness as the underlying type of the expression
description: The value of a complex expression should only be cast to a type that is narrower and of the same signedness as the underlying type of the expressionAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?More InfoIf a cast is to be used on any complex expression, the type of cast that may be applied is severely restricted. As explained in MISRA C 2004, section 6.10, conversions on complex expressions are often a source of confusion and it is therefore wise to be cautious. In order to comply with these rules, it may be necessary to use a temporary variable and introduce an extra statement.Noncompliant code example..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: The value of a complex expression should only be cast to a type that is narrower and of the same signedness as the underlying type of the expression
